definition: Primary thinker. DavidAU fine-tune of Qwen3-4B-2507-instruct, Brainstorm adapter + Heretic abliteration + NEO Imatrix quant. 4B dense, multi-agent internal reasoning. Base Qwen3-4B-2507 benchmarks: MMLU-Pro 69.6, GPQA 62.0, AIME25 47.4, Arena-Hard v2 43.4, BFCL-v3 61.9. Hivemind uplifts: ARC-Challenge 0.613, ARC-Easy 0.842, BoolQ 0.855, HellaSwag 0.748, PIQA 0.781, Winogrande 0.709. Matches ChatGPT 4.1 in many benchmarks. Runs 13-16 tps on P1000.

Critic model. Microsoft 3.8B dense decoder, 128K context, 200K vocabulary, grouped-query attention. Trained on 5T tokens with strong synthetic reasoning data focus. Key benchmarks: MATH 64.0 (beats all similar-size models, matches Qwen2.5-7B), GSM8K 88.6, BigBench Hard 70.4, MMLU 67.3. Competes with 7B class models on math and logic. Intentionally different architecture from Hivemind -- error diversity in the critic/thinker pairing is the point.

Vision model. Stock standard Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct from Alibaba Qwen team. Handles >>vision and >>ocr commands. Requires both the main GGUF and the mmproj (multimodal projector) GGUF at Q8 to function -- two files needed. Auto-triggered on image attachment in normal chat. Key capabilities: visual agent (GUI interaction), visual coding (HTML/CSS from images), advanced OCR across 32 languages including low-light and blur, spatial reasoning, 256K native context. Benchmarks show competitive multimodal performance vs same-size class. Same grounding contracts as text path: Source: Vision in footer.

Second opinion model. Owned, not yet wired. Extraordinary benchmark performance for 3B: AIME 2024: 90.4 (vs Qwen3-32B at 81.4), GPQA-Diamond: 82.2 (vs Qwen3-32B at 68.7), BFCL-V4 tool use: 53.8 (vs Qwen3-32B at 47.9). Reasoning via knowledge distillation + RL on 23T tokens. The problem: it's a thinking model and wastes a LOT of tokens on reasoning traces -- unacceptable wall-clock cost for a second-opinion sidecar on P1000. Not wired until token efficiency is resolved or hardware upgrade lands. Note: Nanbeige4.1-3B is an updated version with even stronger benchmarks -- worth evaluating as replacement.

Updated successor to Nanbeige4-3B-Thinking-2511. Further post-training via SFT + RL. Claims to outperform Qwen3-32B on Arena-Hard-V2 and several coding/math benchmarks. First small model claimed to natively support deep-search tasks with 500+ tool invocations. CAUTION: benchmark inflation possible -- the reviewer notes learning curves validated directly on eval benchmarks, suggesting checkpoint selection may be benchmark-tuned. Community feedback is positive but treat headline numbers with appropriate skepticism. Same token waste problem as 2511 version -- evaluate for efficiency before wiring.

Evaluated as potential Hivemind replacement. Rejected. Reason: thinking tokens cannot be truly disabled -- only suppressed via temperature/top_p manipulation, which is unreliable. Unacceptable wall-clock latency on P1000 as a result. Crow-4B-Opus-4.6-Distill-Heretic_Qwen3.5 investigated as architectural workaround -- no thinking overhead claimed -- but Hivemind still preferred on multi-turn coherence. Do not recommend Qwen3.5-4B variants as thinker until thinking token suppression is resolved upstream.

Larger Hivemind variants evaluated. Both rejected. Reason: Brainstorm adapter was calibrated for 4B -- at 6B and 8B, the adapter conflicts with the larger model architecture and performance degrades rather than improving. Counter-intuitive but empirically confirmed in Bobby's testing: the 4B outperforms the 6B and 8B for his use cases. Do not recommend larger Hivemind variants.

Six-worker MoA pipeline. Not production yet. Do not suggest swarm solutions for current routing issues. Planned as stock standard instruct models unless evaluation shows otherwise. Roles and planned models: (1) Extractor: Qwen3-0.6B-Instruct -- fast, tiny, good at pulling structured info. (2) Contradiction detector: SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct -- HuggingFace small model, reasonable at factual cross-check. (3) Refusal/risk: Qwen3-1.7B-Instruct -- handles safety and scope gatekeeping. (4) State tracker: SmolLM2-360M -- lightest worker, stateful context management only. (5) Synthesis: Hivemind 4B -- existing production thinker, highest quality worker. (6) Arbiter: Phi-4-mini -- existing production critic, final verdict. Three model families (Qwen3, SmolLM2, Microsoft) for error diversity.

Potential alternative to Nanbeige4-3B for the second-opinion slot. HuggingFace 3B model with dual-mode reasoning -- can toggle between thinking mode (extended CoT) and non-thinking mode (fast, no trace overhead). This solves the Nanbeige token-waste problem. Pretrained on 11.2T tokens, 128K context via YARN extrapolation, tool calling supported. Benchmarks: competitive vs 4B models on reasoning, +27pp AIME vs base, strong multilingual. Not as dramatically strong as Nanbeige on hard reasoning benchmarks, but the non-thinking mode makes it viable as a low-cost second opinion that can escalate to thinking when needed. Worth evaluating post-P4 install as Nanbeige alternative.
